location.origin: Stuttgart, Germany -- born 1972 to a French mother and African American father (U.S. Army); first artist in the engine born outside the United States; moved to Great Falls, Montana at age 5
location.formative: Great Falls, Montana (childhood -- isolation incubated omnivorous musical appetite); Cornish College of the Arts, Seattle (jazz piano and acting -- third Cornish connection, first student); Seattle performance art/comedy/music scene (2000s); performed with Wayne Horvitz (degree 2)
location.career: Seattle (formative scene) to national (Comedy Bang! Bang! on IFC; Late Late Show with James Corden on CBS 2015-2023; TED talks; comedy specials); currently NYC/LA based; Seattle remains formative city

theory_nodes.concept: Loop-based composition and beatboxing as biological sound synthesis (voice as universal sound generator -- drum synthesis via vocal tract resonances, vocal bass lines, vocal harmony, vocal melody; loop station as real-time multitrack recorder; one person one microphone one loop pedal creating entire worlds; Amiga Principle at absolute minimum resources)
examples: Reggie Watts -- live loop-based performances: beatbox rhythm loop (drum track) plus vocal bass (bass track) plus harmonic fragments (keyboard track) plus melody/speech (lead track), all built in real-time with zero editing; each performance unique
Bill Frisell (degree 1) -- delay pedal looping: guitar tones layered into contemplative atmospheres; analog continuous decay vs. Watts's digital persistent loops (comparison)
Jeff Lorber (degree 16) -- synthesizer layers: Rhodes/Minimoog/DX7 creating electronic textures; electronic instruments vs. Watts's biological synthesis (comparison)
Bobby McFerrin -- vocal percussion and extended vocal technique; the vocal-as-instrument precedent (context)
Rahzel -- beatbox as stand-alone musical form; hip-hop vocal percussion tradition (context)
Imogen Heap -- live looping with voice and electronics; technology-augmented vocal performance (context)
theory_nodes.description: Loop-based composition using beatboxing and vocal synthesis represents the convergence of three threads: (1) the body-as-sound-source thread taken to its extreme -- the voice as universal sound generator, producing kick drums (bilabial plosive with subharmonic chest resonance), snare (alveolar clicks with aspirated fricatives), hi-hats (dental fricatives), bass lines, chord pads, and melodies, all from a single vocal tract; (2) the loop pedal lineage from Frisell (degree 1) -- technology as multiplier, capturing and layering fragments in real-time; (3) the Amiga Principle at absolute minimum resources -- one person, one microphone, one loop pedal, maximum output complexity. The composition method mirrors multitrack studio production but with zero post-production: every loop is a single take, mistakes become features, imperfections become texture. This is the purest form of improvisation-as-composition in the engine. Seventeenth chromaticism deployment: vocal-tract chromaticism -- continuously variable pitch and timbre produced by sliding between articulatory positions, creating chromatic pitch movement without discrete semitone steps.

ancestors: Loop/delay pedal -- Frisell (degree 1); technology as multiplier creating atmospheres
Synthesizer integration -- Lorber (degree 16); electronic layers as composition
Vocal jazz -- Anderson (degree 3); the voice as primary instrument
Body-as-sound-source thread -- all prior degrees; instruments as extensions of the body
Cornish College of the Arts -- Priester (degree 7 faculty), Caliman (degree 9 faculty); Watts is first student
genealogy.relationship: Loop-based beatbox composition descends from the loop/delay lineage (Frisell degree 1) plus the vocal tradition (Anderson degree 3) plus the body-as-sound-source thread (all degrees). Watts collapses the distinction between instrument and instrumentalist -- the body IS the instrument, AND the synthesizer, AND the drum machine. The eleventh instrument in the body-as-sound-source thread, and the most radical. The Amiga Principle reaches its minimum resource count (one body, one mic, one loop pedal) with maximum output complexity. Keyboard/piano branch not extended (Watts's Cornish training was jazz piano but his performance identity is vocal/beatbox).

theory_nodes.concept: Genre superposition -- Jazz/Comedy as simultaneous coexistence of music and comedy at full intensity without subordination (the refusal to categorize IS the statement; second genre shift after Lorber's Jazz Fusion at degree 16; qualitatively different -- compound genre vs. absence of genre; eighteenth artist-city pattern: The Uncategorizable)
examples: Reggie Watts (degree 17) -- Jazz/Comedy: simultaneously making music and doing comedy at full intensity; neither subordinate to the other; the slash is a superposition, not a fusion; each performance traverses jazz harmony, hip-hop rhythms, electronic textures, spoken-word poetry, and physical comedy in superposition
Jeff Lorber (degree 16) -- Jazz Fusion: first compound genre label; genres merged into a new whole; fusion is a recognized genre with a record-store section (comparison -- fusion vs. superposition)
Aaron Parks (degree 14) -- post-genre: floating between genres unconsciously; genre-crossing as personal style rather than categorical refusal (comparison)
Wayne Horvitz (degree 2) -- composed improvisation crossing jazz/funk/rock/chamber; genre-bridging through composition (comparison)
theory_nodes.description: Genre superposition is the theoretical framework for understanding Watts's Jazz/Comedy classification. Unlike fusion (where two genres merge into a recognized new genre), superposition describes the simultaneous coexistence of two modes (music and comedy) at full intensity without merging or subordination. The slash in Jazz/Comedy is not a fusion operator but a superposition operator. This is the second genre shift in the engine after Lorber's Jazz Fusion (degree 16), but qualitatively different: Lorber shifted from one genre to a compound genre (still classifiable); Watts shifts from genre to the absence of genre (unclassifiable). The genre-crossing is deliberate and simultaneous as a compositional strategy -- within a single performance, Watts traverses jazz harmony, hip-hop rhythms, electronic textures, spoken-word poetry, and physical comedy, not in sequence but in superposition. The refusal to categorize IS the musical and artistic statement. Eighteenth artist-city pattern: The Uncategorizable -- an artist whose primary structural contribution is the demonstration that genre boundaries are arbitrary constructs.

ancestors: Jazz Fusion -- Lorber (degree 16); first compound genre label, first genre shift
Post-genre -- Parks (degree 14); floating between genres unconsciously
Genre-bridging -- Horvitz (degree 2); crossing jazz/funk/rock/chamber through composition
Cross-genre bridge-building -- Jones (degree 0) jazz to pop/R&B/film; Frisell (degree 1) jazz to Americana/folk
genealogy.relationship: Genre superposition is the third stage in the engine's genre evolution: (1) single genre (degrees 0-15, all labeled 'Jazz' with subgenre variations), (2) compound genre (degree 16, 'Jazz Fusion' -- genres merged into recognized combination), (3) superposition (degree 17, 'Jazz/Comedy' -- genres coexisting without merging into a recognized category). The progression from single to compound to superposition maps the continuous loosening of genre boundaries as the circle rotates. Watts is the terminal point of the jazz arc's genre dissolution.

theory_nodes.concept: Cornish College pipeline -- three members, two modes (faculty and student), maximum stylistic range (experimental jazz trombone, hard bop saxophone, performance-art beatbox looping); first student inverts the pipeline to bidirectional; first interdisciplinary training (jazz piano + acting)
examples: Julian Priester (degree 7) -- Cornish faculty; experimental jazz trombone; taught INTO the institution after established career
Hadley Caliman (degree 9) -- Cornish faculty; hard bop saxophone; taught INTO the institution after established career
Reggie Watts (degree 17) -- Cornish student; jazz piano and acting; drew FROM the institution before career began; first student, first interdisciplinary member
Berklee College of Music pipeline (degrees 0, 1, 16) -- three graduates, divergence engine, maximum stylistic spread (comparison)
University of Washington pipeline (degrees 13, 14, 15) -- three graduates, convergence engine, teacher-student relationships (comparison)
theory_nodes.description: The Cornish College of the Arts pipeline deepens to three members at degree 17, gaining its first student after two faculty. The pipeline now operates bidirectionally: Priester (7) and Caliman (9) taught INTO Cornish (bringing expertise to the institution); Watts (17) drew FROM Cornish (taking the institution's training into a career). The stylistic range is extraordinary: experimental jazz trombone (Priester), hard bop saxophone (Caliman), performance-art beatbox looping (Watts) -- three instruments, three genres, three decades. Watts is also the first interdisciplinary Cornish member, studying both jazz piano and acting. The acting training is structural to his career: his performances are theatrical, deploying acting skills (audience interaction, physical comedy, character work) in a musical context. Three institutional pipelines now documented at three members each: Berklee (divergence model, no interaction), UW (convergence model, teacher-student), Cornish (bidirectional model, faculty-then-student). Twentieth educational pipeline documented in the engine: Cornish-to-Seattle-performance-art path.

ancestors: Cornish College -- Priester (degree 7 faculty), Caliman (degree 9 faculty)
Berklee College -- Jones (degree 0), Frisell (degree 1), Lorber (degree 16); divergence model comparison
University of Washington -- Seales (degree 13), Parks (degree 14), Marriott (degree 15); convergence model comparison
genealogy.relationship: Three institutional pipelines now at three members each: Berklee (3 artists, divergence -- no interaction between graduates, maximum stylistic spread), UW (3 artists, convergence -- teacher-student relationships, adjacent degrees), Cornish (3 artists, bidirectional -- two faculty teaching in, one student learning from). Cornish is the most diverse in mode (faculty + student) and the widest in stylistic range relative to institutional size. Cornish College of the Arts name is significant: college of the arts (plural), enabling interdisciplinary training (Watts's jazz piano + acting). Twentieth educational pipeline: Cornish-to-Seattle-performance-art.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- vocal glide (seventeenth deployment; continuous vocal pitch glides approaching scale tones from below or above without landing; zero mechanical intermediary; the most direct blue note mechanism in the engine -- intent to sound with no instrument mediating)
examples: Reggie Watts (degree 17) -- vocal pitch glides approaching scale tones from below or above; continuous pitch movement; zero mechanical intermediary between intent and sound
Jeff Lorber (degree 16) -- synthesizer pitch bend wheel; electronic blue note mechanism; first non-acoustic deployment (comparison)
Thomas Marriott (degree 15) -- half-valve bend on trumpet; assertive brass blue note at energy-3 (comparison)
Bill Frisell (degree 1) -- string bend on guitar; deconstructionist blue note (comparison)
Ernestine Anderson (degree 3) -- vocal blue note; the prior vocal deployment, but Anderson's was within jazz vocal tradition; Watts's extends beyond jazz into beatbox and comedy vocal styles
theory_nodes.description: Seventeenth deployment of the blue note thread. The vocal glide is the most direct blue note mechanism in the engine: continuous pitch movement produced by the vocal tract alone, with zero mechanical intermediary between the performer's intent and the resulting sound. Unlike the synthesizer pitch bend (degree 16, electronic), the guitar string bend (degree 1, mechanical), or the trumpet half-valve (degree 15, acoustic-mechanical), the vocal glide requires no instrument to translate the performer's body movement into pitch change. The voice IS the instrument and the pitch-bending mechanism simultaneously. This is the second vocal blue note deployment (after Anderson, degree 3), but Watts's deployment is structurally different: Anderson's vocal blue note operated within jazz vocal tradition (melisma, bent notes in a ballad context); Watts's operates across beatbox, improvised singing, and comedy vocal styles -- the blue note as a universal human expression mechanism transcending genre. At energy-3, the deployment is performative-explosive: the blue note drives physical comedy and audience engagement, not just musical expression.

ancestors: Blue note -- all sixteen prior deployments (degrees 0-16)
Blue note -- synthesizer pitch bend (degree 16, Lorber); the immediately preceding deployment, first electronic mechanism
Blue note -- vocal (degree 3, Anderson); the first vocal deployment
Blue note -- guitar string bend (degree 1, Frisell); the first string-instrument deployment
genealogy.relationship: Seventeen deployments across eighteen degrees. The vocal glide returns the blue note thread to the voice after fourteen intervening instrumental deployments (degrees 4-16). Seventeen mechanisms: bass-line (0), guitar (1), vocal (3), trumpet-warm (4), piano crush (5), altered voicings (6), trombone slide (7), bass fundamental (8), sax voice (9), alto cool bend (10), flute bent (11), bebop chromatic (12), modal integration (13), post-genre floating (14), assertive brass (15), synthesizer pitch bend (16), vocal glide (17). The vocal glide is the most direct mechanism -- zero intermediary. Second vocal deployment, but in a radically different context (jazz vocal vs. beatbox/comedy/performance art).

seattle_connection: Born Stuttgart, Germany 1972. Moved to Great Falls, Montana at age 5. Studied jazz piano and acting at Cornish College of the Arts in Seattle -- third Cornish connection (after Priester degree 7 faculty, Caliman degree 9 faculty), first Cornish student. Emerged from Seattle's performance art, comedy, and music scenes in the 2000s. Performed with Wayne Horvitz (degree 2) and other Seattle musicians. Became bandleader on The Late Late Show with James Corden (CBS, 2015-2023) and Comedy Bang! Bang! (IFC). The first artist in the engine who CANNOT be categorized -- Jazz/Comedy is not a recognized genre. Genre superposition: music and comedy at full intensity simultaneously without subordination. Loop pedal lineage from Frisell (degree 1) to Watts (degree 17) brackets the first quadrant. Voice as universal sound generator: beatbox as drum synthesis, vocal bass, vocal harmony, vocal melody. Amiga Principle at absolute minimum resources: one person, one microphone, one loop pedal. Third consecutive energy-3 artist (15-16-17). Distribution 2:13:3. First artist born outside the United States. Cornish pipeline deepens to three members, gains first student, becomes bidirectional. Eighteenth artist-city pattern: The Uncategorizable.

theory_genealogy: Loop-based beatbox composition (voice as universal sound generator -- biological drum synthesis, vocal bass, harmony, melody; loop station as real-time multitrack recorder; Amiga Principle at absolute minimum resources) -- body-as-sound-source thread ELEVEN instruments; eleventh the most radical (voice used not as voice but as universal generator); seventeenth chromaticism deployment (vocal-tract chromaticism)

Genre superposition (Jazz/Comedy as simultaneous coexistence without merging -- second genre shift, qualitatively different from Lorber's fusion; the refusal to categorize IS the statement) -- eighteenth artist-city pattern: The Uncategorizable; genre evolution now three stages (single, compound, superposition)

Cornish College pipeline (three members: Priester 7 faculty, Caliman 9 faculty, Watts 17 student -- bidirectional, first student, first interdisciplinary, maximum stylistic range) -- twentieth educational pipeline (Cornish-to-Seattle-performance-art); three pipelines at three members each (Berklee divergence, UW convergence, Cornish bidirectional)

Blue note -- seventeenth deployment; vocal glide (zero mechanical intermediary, most direct mechanism in the engine; second vocal deployment after Anderson degree 3 but in radically different context)
immediate_items: Genre rotation: EIGHTEEN consecutive jazz/jazz-adjacent artists. Second genre shift at degree 17 (Jazz/Comedy). Genre label now includes a non-music discipline. Three-stage genre evolution: single (0-15), compound (16), superposition (17). Track subsequent genre positions.

Second genre shift: Jazz/Comedy is qualitatively different from Jazz Fusion. Superposition (coexistence without merging) vs. fusion (merging into new whole). Track genre dissolution as circle rotates.
Central District geographic oscillation: CD absent at 12-17. SIX consecutive absences after return at 11. Track next CD appearance.
Cornish pipeline: THREE artists (Priester 7, Caliman 9, Watts 17). EXTENDED. Now bidirectional (faculty + student). Tied with Berklee and UW at three members each.
Gender dimension: two women in eighteen degrees (Anderson 3, Bown 6). Sixteen men. CRITICAL. Twelve consecutive male artists (7-17). ESCALATED.
Bebop genealogy: root at 12, modal at 13, post-genre at 14, hard bop trumpet at 15, fusion keyboard at 16, performance-art vocal at 17 -- tree continues widening. Track branch positions.
Addiction-and-recovery thread: one instance (Lanphere 12). Not applicable to Watts. Track subsequent instances.

Energy-3 cluster: THREE members (Marriott 15, Lorber 16, Watts 17). THREE consecutive -- longest streak. Three subtypes: acoustic-assertive, electronic-propulsive, performative-explosive. Distribution 2:13:3. Track escalation.
Energy-2 cluster: thirteen members. Stable. Still dominant but energy-3 closing.
Energy-1 cluster: two members (Peacock 8, Lanphere 12). Stable.
Origin Records label thread (from 15): one artist (Marriott 15). Not applicable to Watts. Track.

UW three-product pipeline: anchor (13), export (14), builder (15). Not applicable to Watts (Cornish pipeline). Track.
Trumpet arc (from 15): Standifer (4) to Marriott (15). Not applicable to Watts (vocal/beatbox). Track.
Talent Incubator archetype (from 16): one instance (Lorber). Not applicable to Watts. Track.
Synthesizer integration thread (from 16): one instance (Lorber). Watts uses voice instead of synthesizers -- biological alternative. Track electronic instrument thread.
Smooth jazz production aesthetic (from 16): fourth production position. Not applicable to Watts (live/unproduced). Track.
Metric modulation thread (from 16): one instance (Lorber). Track.
Funk rhythm thread (from 16): one instance (Lorber). Watts's beatbox rhythms connect to funk tradition but through vocal percussion rather than instruments. Track.
Portland-Seattle corridor (from 16): one instance (Lorber). Watts is Great Falls, Montana to Seattle. Track.
Berklee divergence engine (from 16): three graduates (0, 1, 16). Not applicable to Watts (Cornish). Now tied with Cornish and UW at three members.
Kenny G mentor-protege thread (from 16): one instance. Not applicable to Watts. Track.

Commercial-artistic tension thread (from 16): Watts operates outside the commercial-artistic tension entirely -- his work is neither commercially driven nor critically attacked for commercial concessions.
Horvitz collaboration thread (NEW at 17): first direct collaboration between S36 degree artists documented in the engine (Watts performed with Horvitz).
First non-US birth (NEW at 17): Stuttgart, Germany. Military-base origin. Track subsequent non-US births.
Performance art thread (NEW at 17): first artist whose primary mode is performance art rather than pure music. Comedy as artistic medium. Track subsequent performance art artists.
Late-night bandleader thread (NEW at 17): national television presence (Late Late Show 2015-2023). The most visible musician-role in American culture. Track subsequent television musicians.
Loop pedal lineage (EXTENDED at 17): Frisell (1, delay) to Watts (17, loop station). Brackets the first quadrant. Analog decay vs. digital persistence.

The Uncategorizable archetype (NEW at 17): eighteenth artist-city pattern. First artist in the engine who cannot be classified within existing genre taxonomy. Track subsequent uncategorizable artists.
Dual-discipline training (NEW at 17): jazz piano + acting at Cornish. First formally interdisciplinary artist. Track subsequent dual-discipline artists.
Monk Competition thread: one instance (Parks 14). Not applicable. Track.

Little Big post-genre case study: one instance (Parks 14). Track.

pattern_items: Artist-city taxonomy: eighteen named patterns (Export, Draw, Exile, Stay, Teacher, Persistence, Erasure, Pedagogue-Draw, Quiet Draw, Working Pedagogue-Draw, Late Homestead, Salon Keeper, The Prodigal, The Institutional Anchor, The Prodigy-Export, The Builder-Stayer, The Talent Incubator, The Uncategorizable). Classify every degree.

Educator spectrum: eight modes (institutional, bandstand, self-taught, performance, salon, elder-as-living-archive, university scholar-performer, professional-platform incubator). Watts is primarily a performer, not educator. Track.
Composition-improvisation spectrum: thirteen positions. Watts at the extreme improvisation end -- no scores, no rehearsal, each performance unique. New position added.
Rhythmic spectrum: thirteen positions (beatbox vocal percussion and loop-based rhythm construction added at degree 17).
Chromaticism thread: seventeen deployments. Vocal-tract chromaticism added at degree 17.
Blue note thread: seventeen deployments across eighteen degrees. Vocal glide -- zero mechanical intermediary. Second vocal deployment.
Artist-as-institution spectrum: eighteen positions. The Uncategorizable added.
Keyboard/piano theory branch: ten nodes at degree 16. NOT EXTENDED at degree 17 (Watts's primary identity is vocal/beatbox, not piano). Still richest branch.
Brass theory branch: four nodes at degree 15. Stable (not extended at 17).
Saxophone/reed theory branch: seven nodes. Stable.
Flute theory branch: two nodes. Stable.
Bass theory branch: three nodes. Stable.

Vocal theory branch: EXTENDED to seven nodes at degree 17 (beatbox as drum synthesis, loop-based vocal composition, vocal-tract chromaticism, vocal-glide blue note added). Rivals saxophone/reed branch.
Body-as-sound-source thread: ELEVEN instruments (beatbox/voice-as-universal-generator added). First deployment where the body replaces ALL other instruments simultaneously.
Amiga Principle gradient: thirteen scales. MINIMUM RESOURCE position reached at degree 17 (one person, one mic, one loop pedal). Maximum leverage ratio.
Educational pipeline diversity: twenty pipelines across eighteen degrees. Cornish-to-Seattle-performance-art path added at degree 17.
Formal/informal pedagogy axis: eight modes. Watts is primarily a performer/entertainer.
Gender-barrier gradient: two women, sixteen men in eighteen degrees. CRITICAL. Twelve consecutive male artists (7-17). ESCALATED.
Jackson Street corridor: Watts is post-corridor era and not a Seattle native (Great Falls, MT). No direct corridor connection.

Label threads: Mercury (0, 3), Columbia (6), ECM (1, 7, 8, 14), Catalyst/Mainstream (9), Pacific Jazz (10), local (4, 5, 11), Fresh Sound (12), Blue Note (12, 14), Naim (13), Ropeadope (14), Origin (15), Arista (16), Verve (16), Shanachie (16), various (17 -- no specific label thread). Watts has no strong label affiliation -- consistent with The Uncategorizable pattern.
Electronic jazz waves: four waves (from 16). Watts's loop station is electronic but his primary instrument (voice) is biological -- hybrid position.
Zen thread: from degree 8. Not applicable to Watts. Continue tracking.
Energy spectrum: 2:13:3. THREE consecutive energy-3 artists. Three subtypes documented. Longest energy-3 streak.
Cornish College pipeline: THREE artists (7, 9, 17). EXTENDED. Now bidirectional. Tied with Berklee and UW at three members.
West Coast / LA pipeline: three artists (8, 9, 10). Stable.

Within-family groups: saxophone pair (9/10), flute pair (10/11), keyboard quintet (5/6/13/14/16), trumpet pair (4/15), vocal pair (3/17 -- NEW). Vocal pair added: Anderson (3) and Watts (17) are the engine's two voice-primary artists.
Central District oscillation: present (0-6), absent (7-10), returns (11), absent (12-17). Six consecutive absences. Longest absence streak. Track.
Bebop genealogy tree: root (12), modal (13), post-genre (14), hard bop trumpet (15), fusion keyboard (16), performance-art vocal (17). Tree widens further.
Commercial-artistic tension: from degree 16. Watts operates outside the tension entirely.
Performance art thread (NEW at 17): first artist whose primary mode is performance art. Track.
Late-night bandleader thread (NEW at 17): Watts as Late Late Show bandleader (2015-2023). Track.
Horvitz collaboration thread (NEW at 17): first direct artist-to-artist collaboration documented. Track.
First non-US birth (NEW at 17): Stuttgart, Germany. Track.
Loop pedal lineage (EXTENDED at 17): Frisell (1) to Watts (17). Brackets first quadrant.
The Uncategorizable archetype (NEW at 17): first instance. Track.
Dual-discipline training (NEW at 17): jazz piano + acting. First interdisciplinary formal training. Track.

key_albums.title: Why Shit So Crazy?
key_albums.label: Comedy Central
key_albums.status: debut comedy special -- establishes performance-art identity
key_albums.significance: First major nationally distributed comedy special. Establishes Watts's performance identity: beatboxing, looping, improvised music, and comedy woven together in a continuous performance. Comedy Central release positions Watts in the comedy industry, not the music industry -- the genre superposition begins here.

key_albums.title: Reggie Watts: Spatial
key_albums.label: Netflix
key_albums.status: Netflix special -- loop-based performance art at scale
key_albums.significance: Netflix comedy special filmed in a custom-designed spatial audio environment. Demonstrates the loop-based composition method at its most technically sophisticated. The production emphasizes the musical side of Watts's work while maintaining the comedy framework. Netflix platform provides global audience scale.

key_albums.title: The Late Late Show with James Corden (bandleader)
key_albums.label: CBS Television
key_albums.status: eight-year national television presence as bandleader
key_albums.significance: Eight years as late-night bandleader on CBS -- the most sustained national television presence of any artist in the S36 engine. Late-night bandleaders (Paul Shaffer, The Roots, Jon Batiste) are among the most visible musicians in American culture. Watts brought beatboxing, looping, and performance art to network television nightly. The role demonstrated that his uncategorizable art could function within the most mainstream format in American entertainment.

degree_neighbors.name: Bill Frisell
degree_neighbors.relationship: Loop/delay pedal lineage. Both use technology to multiply a single voice into an ensemble. Frisell: delay pedals creating contemplative atmospheres. Watts: loop station creating absurdist collages. Same principle (technology as multiplier), opposite aesthetic (contemplation vs. absurdism). Brackets the first quadrant: Frisell at degree 1, Watts at degree 17.

degree_neighbors.name: Wayne Horvitz
degree_neighbors.relationship: Direct collaboration and community. Watts performed with Horvitz in Seattle's experimental music scene. Horvitz's infrastructure (Royal Room, curatorial programming) directly supported artists like Watts. The artist-as-institution pattern (degree 2) enabled the emergence of the uncategorizable artist (degree 17). First direct artist-to-artist collaboration documented in the engine.

degree_neighbors.name: Julian Priester
degree_neighbors.relationship: Cornish College pipeline. Third Cornish connection. Priester as faculty (experimental jazz trombone); Watts as student (performance-art beatbox). Pipeline now spans two modes (faculty and student) and three genres. First student inverts the pipeline to bidirectional.

degree_neighbors.name: Hadley Caliman
degree_neighbors.relationship: Cornish College pipeline. Caliman as faculty (hard bop saxophone); Watts as student (jazz piano and acting). Pipeline inverts: Caliman taught INTO the institution; Watts drew FROM it. Three members, maximum stylistic range.

degree_neighbors.name: Jeff Lorber
degree_neighbors.relationship: Genre shift sequence. Lorber's Jazz Fusion (degree 16) softened the genre boundary; Watts's Jazz/Comedy (degree 17) obliterates it. Compound genre vs. absence of genre. Also: synthesizer vs. voice -- Lorber used electronic instruments to create layers; Watts uses his body without external instruments (except loop station as capture device).

garfield_thread.degree_0: Quincy Jones -- attended late 1940s, class of 1950. Arranger/producer. Left Seattle.
garfield_thread.degree_3: Ernestine Anderson -- attended late 1940s, same era as Jones. Vocalist. Left and returned.
garfield_thread.degree_4: Floyd Standifer -- Central District post-WWII, educational path not confirmed as Garfield. Multi-instrumentalist and educator. Never left.
garfield_thread.degree_5: Overton Berry -- Central District native, self-taught. No institutional music education. Autodidact path.
garfield_thread.degree_6: Patti Bown -- Central District native, Seattle University (not Garfield). Formal training via Jesuit university.
garfield_thread.degree_7: Julian Priester -- NOT a Garfield product. DuSable High School (Chicago) under Captain Dyett. Cornish College of the Arts faculty.
garfield_thread.degree_8: Gary Peacock -- NOT a Garfield product. Burley, Idaho. Westlake College (LA). U.S. Army (Germany).
garfield_thread.degree_9: Hadley Caliman -- NOT a Garfield product. Idabel, Oklahoma. Los Angeles jazz scene. Cornish College of the Arts faculty.
garfield_thread.degree_10: Bud Shank -- NOT a Garfield product. Dayton, Ohio. University of North Carolina. Stan Kenton Orchestra. Los Angeles / Pacific Jazz.
garfield_thread.degree_11: Joe Brazil -- NOT a Garfield product. Detroit, Michigan. Detroit jazz scene. Settled in Central District as salon-model educator.
garfield_thread.degree_12: Don Lanphere -- NOT a Garfield product. Wenatchee, Washington. Northwestern University. NYC 52nd Street bebop scene.
garfield_thread.degree_13: Marc Seales -- NOT confirmed as Garfield product. Seattle native. University of Washington (DMA). Seventh consecutive non-Garfield degree. First UW-complete pipeline.
garfield_thread.degree_14: Aaron Parks -- NOT confirmed as Garfield product. Born Seattle 1983. UW pre-college at 14, MSM at 16. Eighth consecutive non-Garfield degree. Exported before completing high school in Seattle.
garfield_thread.degree_15: Thomas Marriott -- NOT confirmed as Garfield product. Born Seattle 1979. UW jazz studies under Seales. Ninth consecutive non-Garfield degree. Stayed in Seattle, built career locally via Origin Records.
garfield_thread.degree_16: Jeff Lorber -- NOT a Garfield product. Born Philadelphia, PA. Berklee College of Music. Portland, OR then Seattle area. Tenth consecutive non-Garfield degree.
garfield_thread.degree_17: Reggie Watts -- NOT a Garfield product. Born Stuttgart, Germany. Great Falls, Montana. Cornish College of the Arts in Seattle (student, jazz piano and acting). Eleventh consecutive non-Garfield degree. First artist born outside the US.

cultural_patterns.institution_type: the uncategorizable -- born Stuttgart Germany (U.S. Army), raised Great Falls Montana, trained at Cornish College of the Arts Seattle (jazz piano + acting, first interdisciplinary student), emerged from Seattle performance art scene, performed with Wayne Horvitz, became Late Late Show bandleader (CBS 2015-2023); an artist whose primary structural contribution is the demonstration that genre boundaries are arbitrary constructs
